text:
Curb Side / Landside

Visitors can only drop off passengers in this area, but they are not allowed to park or stop for more than 10 minutes; if this time passes, this may result in a fine from the police.
Therefore, visitors could use the car park to avail themselves of the shorter tariff bands for passenger pick-up. Prices are published on the website. The link is available in the FAQ document. We also have a pre-booking parking service where visitors can book a space if they need this service.

Feedback:

Passengers may be fined for long stops in this area; this area is controlled by Dubai Police, so customers are required to contact Dubai Police for further clarification on this.

Services available before the check-in area

Free trolleys are available at the entrance of the departure area

Porter service - no prior reservation required. This is a paid service.

Baggage wrapping and baggage weighing services. This is a paid service.

Feedback:

Passengers need to ask the service provider for fees before using the above services.

If a passenger travels with a minor will need to contact the airline as the requirements for traveling with unaccompanied minors vary by airline. Passengers should visit their airline's website or contact the airline directly for correct information and necessary documents.

Other airlines operating in T1 and T2 

Check-in Counter: The check-in staff (Dnata) is responsible for establishing and verifying that the passengers are carrying proper or authorized travel documents according to their destinations. The staff also tags the luggage and prints the boarding pass.

Early check is not possible for other airlines. the check-in counter is open 3 hrs. before departure time. However,

Early/Home Check-in (Dubai) - Passengers can check their bags and receive boarding passes for their flight from their home or hotel. This service is operated by DUBZ. For details.

Call/WhatsApp this number; 052 382 9090

Please visit this link for more info; https://dubz.com

Emirates airlines- T3

Early Check-in: Passengers can drop off their bags 24 hours before departure, or 12 hours before departure if they are flying to the US, and they can collect their boarding pass. Please contact the airline for more details or passengers can visit this link: https://www.emirates.com/ae/english/manage-booking/online-check-in/
Passengers can choose the check-in option that suits them, whether they can check in online, at the airport or home.

Airport Check-in

There’s a check-in area for each travel class Economy, First, and Business.

First and Business Class passengers have a dedicated building to complete their travel procedures (check-in, immigration, and security)
The check-in staff (Emirates) is responsible for establishing and verifying that the passengers are carrying proper/ authorized travel documents according to their destinations. The staff also tags the luggage and prints the boarding pass

Self-check-in kiosks: Passengers can quickly and easy to use self-check-in and bag drop kiosks at the airport. They can tag their bags, and collect their boarding pass. For more details, please visit the following links:

https://www.emirates.com/ae/english/experience/contactless-journey/

https://www.emirates.com/media-centre/emirates-enhances-airport-experience-with-self-check-in-kiosks-in-dubai/

Check-in online on the Emirates App: Passengers can check in online 48 hours before their flight. For more details, please visit this link:  https://www.emirates.com/ae/english/manage-booking/online-check-in/

Home/Early Check-in (Dubai) - Passengers can check in their bags and receive boarding passes for their flight from their home or hotel. This service is operated by DUBZ. For details.

https://www.emirates.com/ae/english/before-you-fly/dubz-home-checkin/

https://dubz.com/order/hci?airline=emirates

Check-in counter closed -As per the rules and regulations followed at the airport all check-in counters close 1 hour before departure time. It is the passenger’s responsibility to reach the airport at least 3 hours before the flight time to allow adequate time to complete the check-in process.

Even when EK passengers check in online, still need to come to the airport check-in counter so EK staff can verify their documents and issue their boarding pass. Passengers are required to arrive 3 hours before departure.

Long queue at the check-in area - We do experience long queues in this area, especially at peak times, due to an increase in passenger flow. We are working with the relevant department on how to reduce queues.

A long wait in the check-in area resulted in a missing flight - We do experience long queues in the check-in area, especially at peak times, due to an increase in passenger flow. However, it’s the passenger’s responsibility to report to the check-in counter on time to avoid missing their flights. Check-in counters are usually opened 3 hours before flight departure time. If passengers arrive outside the applicable time limit at the check-in counter, no passengers will be accepted.

Documents - Passengers must meet the requirements for every country they are traveling to and transit through. This includes the departure country, the transit country, and their final destination. Airlines have the right to refuse passengers if any document is incomplete. Passengers are required to check travel guidelines and airline requirements on the airline's website and to come to the airport with the appropriate travel documents.

Excess luggage - Checked baggage allowance depends on route and fare type. Luggage allowance depends on the airline class (Economy, First & Business) and destination you travel to. As a normal procedure, luggage allowance will be calculated and weighed at check-in, any extra weight will be considered excess luggage and will require passengers to pay the appropriate charges. Each airline has its policy and procedure regarding baggage allowance and usually, this information is published on airline websites or printed on the tickets.

Any bag left behind near the check-in counter it may have been disposed of by the Dubai Police authorized staff, assuming excess baggage. Passengers can come to the airport and contact the police office for further assistance. Passengers can also visit the Lost & Found office in person to check if the bag was delivered to them. The office is open 24 hours and is located in the same building where they left the bag.

Carrying items in carry-on baggage/checked baggage - All items must comply with safety and security regulations. So, passengers need to contact the airline, as airlines accept/reject items onboard according to standards. These terms and conditions are usually posted on airline websites.

Seat allocation- each airline has its policies and procedures regarding seat allocations and customers' seat allocation requests are fulfilled, if possible, during the check-in process however, there is a fee for select seats, which passengers must pay this fee if required. Passengers can follow up on this directly with the concerned airline for further clarification.

Flight overbooked - it is common practice among airlines to overbook flights to use the maximum capacity of the aircraft since there could be last-minute booking cancellations by the customers. Kindly note that it is the airline’s responsibility to take appropriate action should overbooking becomes an issue as they need to offer alternative travel options to the passengers. So, passengers need to contact the respective airline directly for further clarification in this regard.

Canceled/delayed flight - airlines do face delays and cancellations of their flights due to various operational reasons. Therefore, we would like to request you be in contact with the respective airline for any further clarifications in this regard since each airline has a defined policy for passengers.

Immigration:
The immigration officer is responsible for establishing and verifying that the passengers are carrying proper/ authorized and valid documents.
Passengers can use the Smart gates or Immigration counter to complete the immigration process.

Immigration Counter: Passengers should produce their valid passport or acceptable travel document to an immigration officer for processing; If everything is in order, the passport or the travel document is stamped with an immigration exit security stamp, and the traveler is allowed to leave.

Smart Gates: Passengers registered for the Smart Gates can pass through by simply looking at the green light on top of the camera to verify their biometrics, or they may need to scan their biometric passport in case needed. If everything is in order, the gate will open.

The passenger may be denied travel and offloaded from the flight due to an issue with the person or documents they presented. The matter may be referred to the duty office or other parties for further action depending on the nature of the issue in question. *Highlighted in yellow is FYI – no need to share this info with customers.

Exceeding the period allowed in a country - Overstaying beyond the allowed period in a country is the passenger's responsibility which they need to adhere to avoid possible fines.

Traveling with an expired passport - Many countries have specific requirements for passport validity, and this can vary depending on the country of origin, as well as other factors. Passengers need to check their passports and all requirements with  destination country's embassy and airline to make sure that their passports/documents have appropriate validity to cover their entire trip.

Any Issue Smart Gate issues - Passengers should come personally to Immigration Information Centre located in T3, exit 1 open 24/7, or need to contact the General Directorate of Residency & Foreign Affairs/ GDRFA-D - Call Center (Amer Service)

Toll-free: 8005111 
International: +971 4 313 9999 
Email: amer@gdrfad.gov.ae
Website: https://www.gdrfad.gov.ae/en
Contact Center working Hours available 24/7

Stamp Issue in Passport / (no stamp, incorrect date, Entry/ Exit Reports. etc.) - Passengers should come personally to Immigration Information Centre located in T3, exit 1 open 24/7, or need to contact the General Directorate of Residency & Foreign Affairs/ GDRFA-D - Call Center (Amer Service)

Toll-free: 8005111 
International: +971 4 313 9999 
Email: amer@gdrfad.gov.ae
Website: https://www.gdrfad.gov.ae/en
Contact Center working Hours available 24/7

Security:

The main task of airport security is to keep Dubai’s airports safe, inspect and ensure security at each area at Dubai airports, and the safety and well-being of those who are using the airport.

They are responsible for inspections of departing passengers and transit area -transfer desk.

Feedback:

Screening of passenger personal belongings is a standard procedure as per police authority security check requirements. Every item carried by the passengers is screened and x- rayed, therefore passengers are requested to remove all objects/ items and place them in the boxes to be x-rayed. Further to this, a physical checkup is required in case of an alert from the scanning machine and this is a common practice among Security.

That includes Prosthetic body parts (limbs, hands, legs) which need to be scanned individually regardless of carrying the necessary documentation. Furthermore, passengers may need to undergo a physical search if the officer on duty requires them to do so. However, passengers can request from the officer to be searched in a private room, since those rooms are available in each screening area in our airport.

It is a common practice among airport security to conduct random checks on passengers. As per the Dubai airports security policy and procedures, the officer on duty has the right to conduct a special check on passengers randomly and it’s up to the officer to decide on when/who to check while they are on duty. Any passenger has to go through a random security check if the officer on duty requested to do so. The officer on duty will explain to the passenger why the check is been done should the passenger raise any concerns.

As per the usual proses, passengers should remove all the metallic objects/items and put them on the trays to be x-rayed. The staff at the security points are there to guide and advise the passengers to do so, however, the staff will not touch passenger belongings, and it's the passenger’s responsibility to put the items properly inside the provided tray.

Dubai Airports Security follows the rules for ICAO Regulations related to LAGs (liquids, Aerosols, and Gels) as practiced by all Airports around the world. All LAGs (liquids, Aerosols, and Gels) should be carried with a capacity not greater than 100 ml each. By Dubai Airport Regulations, any LAG material (Liquids, Aerosols & Gels) should be packed in resealable transparent bags. Any item exceeding the standard permitted amount or not packed in standard methods will be confiscated by security.

There are many restrictions on items that can be carried in a handbag which vary according to the countries of travel. Additionally, security regulations in any country are regularly reviewed and changed due to various security reasons. Further to this, a Security officer on duty may confiscate any item, if deemed dangerous, irrespective of any standard rule. It is the passenger’s responsibility to check what are items not allowed to carry in a handbag, before the flight. Usually, airlines offer prohibited item details for their passengers. Additionally, pictograms are displayed before and near the security checkpoints for passenger convenience.

Passengers traveling to UK, USA, and Canada are required to ensure that all personal electronic devices (phones/tablets/laptops, etc.) are been charged and can be switched on before boarding the aircraft. Any item that fails to do so would be confiscated and will not be handed back to the passenger.

Boarding Gates:

It is the area where passengers board the aircraft. The gate number is usually provided when passengers check in for their flight and are printed on the boarding pass. Passengers can also find their gate number on flight information display screens at the airport.

Passengers take the boarding passes on the check-in counters; however, the boarding gates belong to the airlines, and they check the documents and sometimes do a certain checkup on passengers for security reasons. As for transit/layover passengers, once they arrive at the terminal, they will need to go to the transfer desk for the same process.

Departing passengers will receive their boarding passes and complete all travel procedures at the check-in area at ​​the departure area.

Transit passengers can receive a boarding pass and complete all travel procedures at the transfer desk (Connection desk) in the transit area within the airport.

For other airlines operating in (T1 and T2) - Dnata is responsible for checking all passenger documents under airline regulations.

For EK passengers in T3 - Emirates is responsible for checking all passenger documents.

However, some airlines require additional document checks at the boarding gate.

Gate closed- it’s the passenger’s responsibility to report to the boarding gates on time as mentioned in their boarding passes to avoid missing their flights. Boarding Gates are usually closed 20 minutes – 1 hour (depending on the destination and airlines) before Flight’s scheduled departure time. If pax arrive outside the applicable time limit at the Boarding Gate, no passenger will be accepted to the flight.

Gate changed- allocated departure gates during the check-in could change due to various operations. Any changes as such will be reflected in the flight information display screens.

Gate changed and no announcement has been made - Dubai airports have adopted the silent airport policy since 2010 to reduce the noise impact on the environment. Therefore, we do not make announcements in the airports unless it’s necessary to do so due to operational reasons. However, to make sure the operations are not compromised by the reduction of announcements, visual information boards (Information display) are made available throughout the airport at passengers’ disposal and are regularly updated. Therefore, passengers are to take note of any changes that happen to the particular flight in terms of boarding gate changes, delays, etc. which appears in the flight information display screens and act accordingly.

Excess handbag - each airline has its policies and procedures regarding baggage allowances, the size and number of hand luggage that can carry varies according to the class of travel (economy, Business, or First) and certain cases, destination. Some airlines adopt strict policies concerning the amount of kg that can be carried in hand luggage and will weigh the hand luggage before boarding and charge for any excess baggage if it exceeds the permitted allowance. As per the airline policy, if duty-free purchases plus the hand luggage including the laptop bag weight exceeds the permitted allowance, passengers will be charged for the extra amount accordingly. Usually, the baggage allowance details (including the permitted hand luggage allowances) are mentioned on the ticket reservations along with other terms and conditions for passenger information. Passengers are required to adhere to the airline policies and need to contact the airline as each airline has its policy regarding baggage allowance.

Security Machines at the boarding gate - Some destinations impose additional security measures to ensure the safety of passengers. In this area, this procedure will be carried out by Dubai Police upon the request of some airlines.

Airline Security - Certain airline policies require additional document verification, and airlines have the right to question passengers for security purposes. In this area, this procedure will be carried out by the airline security staff upon the request of some airlines.

In some cases, in this area - due to size issue airline staff may request that the bag or stroller be taken and registered as checked baggage and the passenger will be given a tag/receipt for this.

If Pax files a complaint and provide a tag number, this case must be registered as Lost checked luggage through CRM.

If Pax say no tag/receipt was given by airline staff in this area - this should be checked with the Lost and Found department and Baggage Services as well.

Upon arrival at the destination, passengers did not receive their luggage - make sure that passengers provide the following details before sending feedback to the respective baggage service.

Baggage tag no

Claim reference no (if a missing bag report was raised upon arrival with baggage service)

Flight No/Airline

Date of travel

Upon arrival at the destination, passengers discover that the bag is damaged - Passengers are required to report damage to checked-in baggage/stroller upon arrival with the baggage services / relevant airline - within a certain number of days of arrival. Therefore, Pax need to raise /follow up on the matter with the concerned airline for claims on the same.

Upon arrival at the destination, passengers discover that items are missing from their checked baggage - make sure that passengers provide these items

If these items are (clothes, valuables, or personal items) - Passengers are required to be in contact with the respective Airline since such suspected pilferage is under the liability of the respective Airline. Further, all checked-in baggage and its contents are accepted for carriage by the respective Airline hence the liability and claims/compensation remain with the Airline.

Side Note (Suspected pilferage) meaning: Any cases of pilferage should be referred to the airline, not LPMS (Lost Property Management System). So, if someone reported that an item is missing from their bag, and they suspect it was stolen, this should be    referred to the airline. LPMS is not for handling such cases.

If Pax mentions in feedback that he has found a Baggage Inspection Notice inside the bag - as per the security rules and procedures, all checked-in bags that are passing through Dubai Airport, are subjected to screening and inspection. Some bags are selected and opened for physical inspection, by the security staff. During the inspection, our security officer will search the bags and their contents for any prohibited items. Upon completion of the inspection process, the prohibited items will be removed, and the other contents will be returned to the bag. If the bag was locked, unfortunately, the security officer will be forced to break the locks to search the bag. The confiscated items are not recoverable.

Visa inquiries:

Customer is in the UAE and is inquiring about visas and entry permits to enter Dubai
Visas and entry permit inquiries are under the responsibility of the Immigration Department. Passengers are required to contact the GDRFAD website https://www.gdrfad.gov.ae/en/services 
Toll-free number: 8005111
Email: amer@gdrfad.gov.ae
Website: https://www.gdrfad.gov.ae/en

They can also come to the airport, and visit the GDRFA Office, Arrival Building 3, Entrance 1.

The customer is outside the UAE and is inquiring about visas and entry permits to enter Dubai
Visas and entry permit inquiries are under the responsibility of the Immigration Department. Passengers are required to contact the GDRFAD: https://www.gdrfad.gov.ae/en/contact-information
Outside U.A.E: +971 4 313 9999
Email: amer@gdrfad.gov.ae
Website: https://www.gdrfad.gov.ae/en

If a Pax insist on having the immigration number as the immigration informed him to contact the airport for this; We can say: Unfortunately, these are the only phone numbers we have available to share. However, you may reach Immigration by visit their website for contact information: https://www.gdrfad.gov.ae/en/contact-information

Passengers inquire if they have a UAE visa issued, for example, from Abu Dhabi or any other emirate in the UAE, and ask if they can enter from Dubai.
Passengers will be able to enter Dubai if the visa is valid, issued, and approved by the relevant authorities in the UAE. Passengers can contact the GDRFAD for more details: https://www.gdrfad.gov.ae/en/contact-information

Passengers from GCC countries asking if they need a visa to enter Dubai
GCC nationals do not require a visa to enter Dubai. Passengers can contact the GDRFAD for more details: https://www.gdrfad.gov.ae/en/contact-information 

Passengers residing in the GCC countries and asking if they need a visa/entry permit to enter Dubai.
If customers are a resident of any of the GCC countries and want to visit the UAE, they can apply for an E-visa online through this website; smartservices.icp.gov.ae

Documents required for GCC residents’ eVisa
• Valid residence visa from one of the GCC countries – the visa should be valid for at least three months.
• Valid passport copy, with the passport valid for at least six months.
• Colored passport-sized photo. Before you upload the passport photo it must meet standards and requirements mandated by ICP (Federal Authority for Identity, Citizenship, Customs & Port Security)

If customers travel with family members, such as spouses or children, then they must provide proof of kinship when applying for their visa.
For fees and more details on this query, passengers should contact the GDRFA at https://www.gdrfad.gov.ae/en/contact-information 

Passengers are asking about visa status or track on visa application
Customers who applied for a Dubai visa or through Amer centers in Dubai need to have the application number and reference number to track the visa application through GDRFA in Dubai https://www.gdrfad.gov.ae/en/contact-information
Toll-free number: 8005111
Outside U.A.E: +971 4 313 9999
Email: amer@gdrfad.gov.ae
Website: https://www.gdrfad.gov.ae/en

Customers can also visit the nearest Customer Happiness Center at Amer Services Center or the GDRFA office

Passengers asking about fines for overstaying their visa
Customers are required to pay AED 50 daily overstay fines for a visit, tourist, and residence visas. Fees are subject to change therefore passengers should contact GDRFA to confirm this inquiry 
https://www.gdrfad.gov.ae/en/contact-information
Toll-free number: 8005111
Outside U.A.E: +971 4 313 9999
Email: amer@gdrfad.gov.ae
Website: https://www.gdrfad.gov.ae/en

Passengers asking if they can pay visa overstay fines at Dubai Airports: Passengers can pay overstay fines at the Immigration counter at all terminals.

GCC passengers are inquiring if they can enter with ID: GCC nationals are allowed to enter Dubai with a national identity card.

Passengers inquire about entry visas to other countries: Customers can contact the embassy of the relevant country at their destination. They can also obtain this information from the airline they are traveling with.

Passengers asking who can use smart gates: Registered passengers with a height of 1.2 meters and above. For more details visit this link: https://www.gdrfad.gov.ae/en/smart-gate-inquiry

Customers can also contact GDRFA(Immigration) https://www.gdrfad.gov.ae/en/contact-information

Passengers are complaining about their inability to use the smart gate
Passengers need to contact GDRFA to resolve the issue as the smart gate is operated by the immigration department. https://www.gdrfad.gov.ae/en/contact-information

Passport renewal:

Can I renew my passport at Dubai Airport?

Only UAE nationals can renew their passports at the GDRFA office at T3, Entrance 1, Arrival Building. 

Non-UAE citizens should contact their respective airlines or the relevant embassy/consulate in the UAE for passport renewal procedures and requirements.

What are the documents required to renew a UAE passport at Dubai Airports?

Bring an expired passport.

Bring the original and copy of the Emirates ID Card.

Personal photo with a white background not exceeding 6 months.

Original and copy of the family book *we call it in UAE (خلاصة القيد أو الجنسية)

How much does it cost to renew a UAE passport?
Renewal prices are 200 AED. However, prices are subject to change

How long does it take to renew a passport?
Passport renewal can take from 30 minutes to 45 minutes.

Pax asked about passport validity for departing from Dubai: Passengers should check the passport and document validity requirements directly on the airline's website. Airlines may have different rules, so passengers must contact the concerned airline directly for accurate information.

Stamp Issue in Passport:

Passengers complain that they discovered that there is no immigration stamp on their passports upon entry or departure
Customers in the UAE can come to the airport by contacting GDRFA office at T3, Entrance 1, Arrival Building Also, passengers can contact the GDRFAD for more details: https://www.gdrfad.gov.ae/en/contact-information

Travelers complain that they discover that the stamp date is incorrect on their passports upon entry or departure
Customers in the UAE can come to the airport visit contact the GDRFA office at T3, Entrance 1, Arrival Building Also, passengers can contact the GDRFAD for more details: https://www.gdrfad.gov.ae/en/contact-information

Passengers asking for a report on entry and exit from Dubai.
Customers in the UAE can come to the airport visit contact the GDRFA office at T3, Entrance 1, Arrival Building Also, passengers can contact the GDRFAD for more details: https://www.gdrfad.gov.ae/en/contact-information

Tourists are asking how to get Al Saada card:
Tourists can follow the steps below

Download the ALSAADA application from Google Play and Apple Store.

Enter the date of arrival and the number of the passport to receive the ALSAADA Card.

Login using your passport number as the username and the password.

To find out more related to the services or benefits provided by the ALSAADA program, please find the contact details.
Mobile: 0564100500
Email: alsaada@gdrfad.gov.ae

Where can tourists get a SIM card in Dubai Airports?
Tourists with a transit visa, visit visa, or visa on arrival, and GCC citizens above the age of 18 can get a complimentary Tourism SIM card from telecom operator du when they cross immigration at DXB.

Private Jet (DXB & DWC)


You can share the below contact details with the customer depending on the customer's request if looking for service or rental or both. 

1- Al Futtaim (Renting / Services) 
Tel: 056 9942004 
Email: ops@dc-aviation.ae

2- Jet Aviation (Services)
Tel: 056 1743146 
Email: dwcfbo@jetaviation.com

3- Executive Jet (Renting / Services) 
Tel: 056 9919740 
Email: Fbo.omdb@execujet-me.com

Value Refunds


Tourists and visitors traveling through Dubai Airports can now refund their VAT amount at the airport, available in all terminals in DXB & DWC.

"Planet” is the company that has been authorized by the Federal Tax Authority to operate the tourist VAT refund scheme on its behalf.

Rules for tax-free shopping

Currency in Dirham AED

Vat Rate 5%

Mini spend 250 AED

Eligibility for a VAT refund – Overseas tourist
Goods that are eligible for tax refunds - All goods are subject to VAT EXCEPT.

Goods that have been fully or partially consumed in the United Arab Emirates or any applicable country

Cars, boats, and planes

Goods not taken by the passenger at the time of leaving the UAE

For full terms and conditions/ issue/ clarification & assistance; please visit www.planetpayment.ae  or Call: +971 45864700

Permits (Royal family, Ambulances, and Diplomatic mission permit)


1- Royal family, Ambulances, and Diplomatic mission permit: Provide customers with the following number of Airport Security Pass Office.

Tel: 045045036 (During official working hours)

Tel: 045045000 (After Working hours and during holidays)

Email: ASP@dubaiairports.ae

Official letter from the hospital including patient information, flight details, driver, and vehicle information.

Request to fill out the Ambulance Car pass application form – Attached below

Patient's passport and visa

Driver license copy, UAE ID,

Vehicle registration card copy

Send the above documents to: PassApproval@dubaiairports.ae

2- Customers want permission to meet the transit passengers: We do not have such meeting areas in our airport to meet transit passengers, due to security reasons.

3- A customer called to request the number of the Executive office, CEO, or Deputy Chief Executive Officer: Provide the number below:

President /CEO office: 04-504 4433

Deputy Chief Executive Officer: Jamal Al Hai: 04-504 4415

SVP/VP contact details We are sorry to inform you that we are not allowed to share contact details, however, you can write your query through the feedback form and we will share your feedback to the concerned person/ department.

Other DA employees We regret to inform you that we are not allowed to share the contact details of our staff with the customers.

Internships: 

Currently, the internship opportunities are available for UAE citizens only, request the customer to go through the link;

https://careers.dubaiairports.ae/en/graduate-internships/

No foreign students are accepted for an internship in Dubai airports as of now.

Free Wi-Fi:

Passengers can now enjoy unlimited, free, high-speed Wi-Fi, across all areas of DXB and DWC. To get connected, just follow the below steps:

Select DXB Free Wi-Fi or DWC Free Wi-Fi from the network list on your Wi-Fi option on your phone.

Open your web browser

Click Get Online now

Lounges:

Dubai airports offer many lounge facilities managed by different stakeholders. https://www.dubaiairports.ae/experiences/relax---refresh

If passenger asks if there are lounges before the security?

Yes, we have lounges before the security and for more information he can visit the link https://www.dubaiairports.ae/experiences/relax---refresh and filter the area "Before the security".

heading_path: For more details, please find the below details
text:
Meet and Greet Related (Free Lounges):

Depending on the airline, class of service, and airport, passengers may only have access to one lounge, or they may have several to choose from. Therefore, passengers need to check eligibility to enter the lounge with the airlines or service providers either for free or for a fee. Passengers can visit the following link to find the lounge available at Dubai airports; https://www.dubaiairports.ae/while-youre-here/relax-directory?filter-category=lounges

Located in concourse D

6- Plaza Premium

School Trips: 

This type of personal requests is not entertained at the airport, due to various reasons.


Flight Status:

Note: We are required to advise the passengers to check the flight status on our website from this link: https://www.dubaiairports.ae/flight-status , after providing him with the requested flight information

Flight delays/cancellations could occur due to various operational and technical reasons.

However, in the event of a flight being canceled/ delayed, it’s the airline’s responsibility to keep the passengers informed accordingly.

Passengers can visit the Dubai Airports website to check the flight status http://www.dubaiairports.ae/flight-status

heading_path: For questions related to flight status: You can ask any question from below
text:
Would you please provide me with the airlines and destination?

Would you please provide me with the name of the airline and the date?

Would you please provide me with the destination and date?

Would you please provide me with the flight number and date?

Note: If a passenger inquiries about the terminal from which a specific airline operates, the DA community app can inform us by using the airline's name and search or DA  website by using this link: https://www.dubaiairports.ae/before-you-fly/which-terminal/airline-directory

Film/ Photography permits inside the airport

All professional/commercial photography and filming at DXB require express permissions/permits depending on the location where the shoot is to be conducted.

Depending on the nature of the shoot (commercial/advertisements/promotions/feature films etc. with or without a production agency involved), it may be necessary for the project to apply for a location permit with the Dubai Film & TV Commission (there is a charge involved).

Taking pictures of the airfield/aircraft and other airport facilities is prohibited and will invite action from local law enforcement. It is best to approach the communication and reputation department with your request and arrange for necessary permissions if possible. https://media.dubaiairports.ae/filming--photography/

Shower Facility:

➢ A Free Shower is available at Terminal 3 at Concourse B and DWC. Passengers can buy toiletries from Dubai Duty-Free.

• T3 located between Gate B13 & Gate B19
• DWC has located next to gate B9 and the mezzanine level beside Marhaba.

Hairdryer is not available in the complimentary shower facilities. However, passengers can visit the Timeless Spa in Terminal 3 https://www.dihdxb.ae/timeless_spa which offers hairdryer services.

They can also check out Be Relax Spa or Sleep 'n Fly Lounge, these facilities may also offer hairdryers. *All facilities mentioned require a fee for this service.

Note: We do not have this free shower facility at Concourse D T1, C T3, and T2. However, request the customer to check with Marhaba Lounge (paid service).

• Marhaba Lounge has shower facilities only in Terminal 1 and Terminal 3 at DXB airport.

For this Pax should have Marhaba lounge booking to book the shower facility upon arrival at the lounge.

It can only be booked on the spot as it's always subject to availability.

➢ Paid Shower

        • Health Club/G-Force located in Dubai International Hotel located at Concourse B. Visit DIH website; https://www.dubaiintlhotels.com
